mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >65% of months where FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) during structural labor shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between retail sales growth and consumer price inflation, where CPIAUCSL rises faster than RSAFS, indicates demand destruction that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict direction with >55% accuracy over 60-day windows, or Rank IC remains below 0.04 across all rolling 2-year samples.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening supply-side capacity that supports corporate margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth coincides with negative 20-day SPX returns in >60% of observations during expansion regimes, or predictive power vanishes when supply bottlenecks dominate.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels exceeding 260 signal entrenched inflation that forces market repricing of equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns are positive in >65% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 260, or the threshold signal yields a negative Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls indicate robust labor demand that sustains consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to predict 60-day SPX direction with >55% accuracy, or rolling Rank IC drops below 0.03 over consecutive 2-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in housing starts and retail sales confirms broad-based domestic demand strength that translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal is followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction effect shows no incremental predictive power over single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: Declining GDP growth below 2.0% quarter-over-quarter signals a supply-constrained slowdown that triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days during late-cycle phases.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >65% of triggered instances during late-cycle regimes, or the signal's Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.
